heading: Improving data accuracy
label: 644 Improving data accuracy
notes: Amendments2021—Subsec. (b)(2)(B), (C). added subpar. (B) and struck out former subpars. (B) and (C) which read as follows:Pub. L. 117–58“(B) not later than 1 year after , conclude a process that tests the feasibility of partnering with Federal agencies that operate delivery fleet vehicles, including the United States Postal Service, to facilitate the collection and submission of information described in that paragraph; andMarch 23, 2020“(C) not later than 14 months after , publish on the website of the Commission, and submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives, a report regarding the testing described in subparagraph (B), which shall include—March 23, 2020“(i) a determination regarding whether the partnerships with Federal agencies described in that subparagraph are able to facilitate the collection and submission of information described in paragraph (1); and“(ii) any steps that the Commission plans to take to facilitate the partnerships described in that subparagraph.”

L. 117–58“(B) not later than 1 year after , conclude a process that tests the feasibility of partnering with Federal agencies that operate delivery fleet vehicles, including the United States Postal Service, to facilitate the collection and submission of information described in that paragraph; andMarch 23, 2020“(C) not later than 14 months after , publish on the website of the Commission, and submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives, a report regarding the testing described in subparagraph (B), which shall include—March 23, 2020“(i) a determination regarding whether the partnerships with Federal agencies described in that subparagraph are able to facilitate the collection and submission of information described in paragraph (1); and“(ii) any steps that the Commission plans to take to facilitate the partnerships described in that subparagraph.”

children.heading: Audits
children.label: a Audits
children.text: The Commission shall conduct regular audits of information submitted to the Commission by providers under to ensure that the providers are complying with this subchapter.section 642(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commission shall develop a process through which entities or individuals in the United States may submit specific information about the deployment and availability of broadband internet access service in the United States on an ongoing basis so that the information may be used to verify and supplement information provided by providers of broadband internet access service for inclusion in the maps created under .section 642(c)(1) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: have proven methodologies for determining network coverage and network performance; and

children.label: B
children.text: coordinate with the Postmaster General, the heads of other Federal agencies that operate delivery fleet vehicles, and the Director of the Bureau of the Census for assistance with data collection whenever coordination could feasibly yield more specific geographic data.

children.heading: Technical assistance to Indian Tribes
children.label: c Technical assistance to Indian Tribes

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the Commission shall hold workshops for Tribal Governments in each of the 12 Bureau of Indian Affairs regions to provide technical assistance with the collection and submission of data under .section 642(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Annual review
children.label: 2 Annual review
children.text: Each year, the Commission, in consultation with Indian Tribes, shall review the need for continued workshops required under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Technical assistance to small service providers
children.label: d Technical assistance to small service providers
children.text: The Commission shall establish a process through which a provider that has fewer than 100,000 active broadband internet access service connections may request and receive assistance from the Commission with respect to geographic information system data processing to ensure that the provider is able to comply with the requirements under in a timely and accurate manner.section 642(b) of this title

children.heading: Technical assistance to State, local, and Tribal governments and consumers
children.label: e Technical assistance to State, local, and Tribal governments and consumers

children.text: the provision of staff of the Commission to provide assistance, as needed, throughout the entirety of the challenge process.

children.heading: GAO Assessment of Fabric source data
children.label: f GAO Assessment of Fabric source data

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct an assessment of key data sources that are used for purposes of the Fabric to identify and geocode locations where fixed broadband internet access service can be installed in order for the Comptroller General to develop recommendations for how the quality and completeness of those data sources can be improved as data sources for the Fabric.

children.label: A
children.text: any relevant sources of Federal data, including the National Address Database administered by the Department of Transportation;

children.label: B
children.text: State- and county-level digitized parcel data; and

children.heading: Report
children.label: 3 Report
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives a report that contains the recommendations developed under paragraph (1).March 23, 2020
